title: In and not in operators should be used on objects supporting them
description: Avoid using 'in' and 'not in' operators on objects that do not support membership testing.
message: Avoid using 'in' and 'not in' operators on unsupported objects.
recommendation: Use ismember and not ismember on objects that support them.

compliant.description: Using 'not in' on a set created from a string

why_is_this_an_issue: Using 'in' and 'not in' operators on unsupported objects may lead to unexpected results or errors.
how_to_fix: Use objects that support membership testing (e.g. lists, sets, tuples, dictionaries, or custom classes with an __contains__ method).

logic.new_check_type_doc: Checks if the left-hand side of the comparison is an object that supports membership testing.

metadata.title: In and not in operators should be used on objects supporting them
